topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a jarring intrusion into a perfectly calm afternoon. A profile picture loaded: a grinning, familiar face I hadn't seen in fifteen years—Mark. My stomach clenched, a tight, unpleasant knot forming beneath my ribs. A friend request. My breath hitched. I stared at the name, my jaw tightening so hard I could feel the muscles strain in my neck. It was impossible. After everything, after the years of distance and mutual avoidance, this was how he resurfaced? I immediately navigated to his profile, my fingers hovering over the 'Decline' button, but a wave of hot, furious energy washed over me. I closed my eyes, taking several deep, ragged breaths, trying to force the sudden, sharp spike of anger down. I needed to process this absurdity.

[story 2]

I almost dropped my phone. The sight of Mark’s name pop up felt like a physical blow. My hands started shaking, a rapid tremor running up my arms. I looked around my room, suddenly hyper-aware of the silence surrounding me, as if the air itself was thick with unspoken tension. Why now? After all the years I’d spent building a life separate from him, this digital reach-out felt like a deliberate provocation. A low growl started deep in my chest, vibrating through my sternum. I snatched the phone back, my thumb hovering over the settings menu. I didn't click anything immediately. Instead, I leaned back against my desk, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, my gaze fixed on the screen with an intensity that bordered on hostile.

[story 3]

When the alert chimed, I froze mid-sip of my coffee. The liquid suddenly felt scalding in my throat. It was him. Mark. The casualness of the request—just a little blue bird icon and the familiar handle—sent a surge of pure, unadulterated irritation through me. I set the mug down with a decisive clatter that echoed in the quiet apartment. My eyes narrowed, the pupils dilating slightly as I scrutinized the profile. A flush crept up my neck, warm and prickly. I stood up abruptly, pacing three steps before spinning back to the screen, my voice coming out tight and low. "What in the world are you doing?" I muttered to the empty room, my fists clenching at my sides until my knuckles turned white.
